text: Water can boil and freeze at the same time — it's called the triple point.
A teaspoon of honey represents the life work of about 12 bees.
Glass is neither a true solid nor a liquid — it's an amorphous solid.
Bananas are naturally slightly radioactive due to their potassium-40 content.
Hot water can freeze faster than cold water — known as the Mpemba effect.
Helium is the only element that cannot be solidified at normal pressure, no matter how cold.
There are more atoms in a glass of water than glasses of water in all the oceans.
Lightning is about five times hotter than the surface of the Sun.
A single bolt of lightning contains enough energy to toast about 100,000 slices of bread.
Sound travels roughly four times faster in water than in air.
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire — teaching began there around 1096.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the building of the Great Pyramid.
The shortest war in history, between Britain and Zanzibar in 1896, lasted under 40 minutes.
Ancient Romans used crushed mouse brains as toothpaste.
Woolly mammoths were still alive when the Great Pyramid of Giza was being built.
Napoleon was once attacked by a horde of thousands of rabbits gathered for a hunt.
The fax machine was invented before the American Civil War, in 1843.
In ancient Egypt, servants were sometimes smeared with honey to attract flies away from the pharaoh.
The Eiffel Tower was originally intended to be dismantled after 20 years.
Vikings never actually wore horned helmets — that image came from 19th-century opera costumes.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
Neutron stars are so dense that a sugar-cube-sized amount would weigh about a billion tons.
There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on all of Earth's beaches.
Footprints left on the Moon will likely remain for millions of years — there's no wind to erase them.
The Sun makes up 99.86% of the mass of our solar system.
One million Earths could fit inside the Sun.
It rains diamonds on Neptune and Uranus.
Space is completely silent — sound waves need a medium to travel through.
Olympus Mons on Mars is nearly three times the height of Mount Everest.
Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth.
Octopuses have three hearts, nine brains, and blue blood.
A group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance'.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping so they don't drift apart.
Cows have best friends and get stressed when separated from them.
A shrimp's heart is located in its head.
Sloths can hold their breath longer than dolphins — up to 40 minutes.
Tardigrades can survive in the vacuum of space.
Elephants are one of the few animals that can recognize themselves in a mirror.
A snail can sleep for up to three years at a time.
Crows can remember human faces and hold grudges for years.
Russia spans 11 time zones.
Africa is the only continent located in all four hemispheres.
Canada has more lakes than the rest of the world combined.
The Sahara Desert is roughly the same size as the entire United States.
Istanbul is the only major city located on two continents.
Australia is wider than the Moon.
The Dead Sea is so salty that people float effortlessly on its surface.
Mount Everest grows about 4 millimeters taller every year.
There's a town in Norway called Hell, and it freezes over every winter.
About 90% of the world's population lives in the Northern Hemisphere.
Your body produces about 25 million new cells every second.
The human brain uses about 20% of the body's total energy.
Your stomach gets a new lining every few days to avoid digesting itself.
Humans are the only animals known to blush.
Your heart beats about 100,000 times a day.
The strongest muscle in the body relative to its size is the masseter — your jaw muscle.
You are about 1 centimeter taller in the morning than at night.
The acid in your stomach is strong enough to dissolve razor blades.
Your bones are about five times stronger than steel of the same density.
Fingernails grow about four times faster than toenails.
